serving.natural_language_portion: One Grande size cup of iced caffè latte is typically 350 grams.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Starbucks Beverage Nutritional Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from Starbucks Iced Caffe Latte data in USDA FoodData Central, as the input data aligns closely with typical commercial preparations.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Milk (Full Cream)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated based on the GI of full cream milk (GI 30), as the primary carbohydrate source in an unsweetened iced latte is lactose from milk. Espresso has a negligible GI.

hypertension.guidance: Unsweetened iced caffè latte is generally suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its low sodium content (28.6 mg/100g) and absence of added sugars; however, caffeine intake should be moderated.

type2_diabetes.guidance: With 3.4g sugar/100g from naturally occurring lactose and 2.3g protein/100g, this beverage has a low glycemic impact and is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugars or syrups.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: An unsweetened iced caffè latte made with regular milk has moderate saturated fat (0.86g/100g) and cholesterol (5.71mg/100g); choosing low-fat or skim milk can further reduce these for cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: An Iced Caffè Latte is a popular cold beverage made by combining dark, rich espresso with cold milk and serving it over ice. It typically features a strong coffee flavor balanced by the creaminess of milk, without added sugar unless specified. The texture is smooth and refreshing, making it a favored drink in warmer climates or as a cool pick-me-up. Nutritionally, it provides protein and calcium from milk, with carbohydrates primarily from naturally occurring lactose. For clinical relevance, unsweetened versions are generally suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension due to low added sugar and sodium, though saturated fat can be a consideration for cardiovascular health depending on the milk type used. It is a common beverage in Western coffee culture and widely available globally.
common_user_queries: Is iced coffee good for you?

confidence_reason: Nutrient data aligns well with typical commercial iced lattes, and GI is estimated from a primary component (milk) from an approved source.
